name: Kasa Smart Plug Mini EP25, Matter Compatible Energy Monitoring
whatWorks: The EP25 includes real-time energy monitoring at $29 CAD, a feature that typically costs $50+ on competing smart plugs. The Kasa app breaks down daily, weekly, and monthly kWh consumption with estimated cost — genuinely useful for identifying energy hogs like space heaters and older appliances.

Matter compatibility means the EP25 works natively with HomeKit, Alexa, Google, and SmartThings without needing a Kasa account for local control. For users building a Matter-first smart home, this plug integrates cleanly with any Matter controller.

The compact form factor doesn't block the second outlet on a standard duplex receptacle — a detail that sounds minor until you've used an oversized smart plug that makes your second outlet unusable. Kasa's flat-profile design solves this correctly.

Setup via the Kasa app takes under two minutes and requires no QR scanning or account linking for basic use. The app's scheduling and away-mode features (random on/off to simulate occupancy) work reliably and are accessible to non-technical users.
worthKnowing: The EP25 requires a 2.4GHz Wi-Fi network — it does not support 5GHz. This is common across smart plugs but worth noting if your router broadcasts only a combined network. Most routers handle this automatically, but some users on Wi-Fi 6E setups need to verify their 2.4GHz band is active.

Matter local control bypasses the Kasa cloud, but initial setup still requires the Kasa app and a Kasa account. After Matter pairing is established, you can control it without the account — but the setup process isn't entirely cloud-free.

There's no USB port on the EP25, unlike some competing smart plugs that add a USB-A or USB-C charging port. If you need to charge a device through the smart plug outlet, the EP25 can do it, but you can't charge via USB separately from the controlled outlet.

The Kasa app occasionally pushes firmware updates that require a brief reconnection period. Most users don't notice, but automations relying on precise timing may miss a trigger during an update window.

commonComplaints.title: No 5GHz support…
commonComplaints.body: My router broadcasts a combined 2.4/5GHz network with the same name and this plug would not connect. Had to dig into router settings to split the bands.

commonComplaints.title: Still need a Kasa account for Matter setup…
commonComplaints.body: I thought Matter meant I could skip creating yet another account. Nope — still had to make a Kasa account just to onboard it. After that, HomeKit works fine though.

commonComplaints.title: No USB charging port…
commonComplaints.body: Every other smart plug I've used has a USB port. This one doesn't. Not a dealbreaker but I miss having it for phone charging.

commonComplaints.title: Firmware updates disconnect briefly…
commonComplaints.body: Lost my lamp automation at 2am three times now due to firmware updates. Wish there was a way to schedule updates for daytime.

verdict: The Kasa EP25 is the best value smart plug in Canada — $29 CAD buys you real-time energy monitoring, Matter compatibility, compact design, and rock-solid Wi-Fi reliability. Competing plugs charge $50–80 for similar feature sets. It's the straightforward answer for anyone who wants to make any outlet smart without overthinking it.

realBuyers.quote: Energy monitoring showed my space heater was costing me $40/month. I had no idea. Already paid for itself.
Connected to HomeKit without issues. My lamp, coffee maker, and fan are all on automations now. Works every time.
Compact design is the big thing — I can use both outlets on the same faceplate. My old smart plug made me lose the second outlet.
Setup was two minutes. I've set up six of these across the house. Zero issues in a year of daily use.

sources.title: Kasa Smart Plug Mini EP25 — Amazon.ca

metaDescription: Kasa EP25 Smart Plug review for Canada: Matter compatible, real-time energy monitoring, compact design, $29. Honest on 2.4GHz limit and setup. ~$29 CAD.
introText: The Kasa Smart Plug Mini EP25 packs real-time energy monitoring and Matter compatibility into a compact outlet form factor at $29 CAD — one of the most feature-rich smart plugs at this price point. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B091FXLMS8) at around $29 CAD.
